mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding economic activity, raising corporate earnings expectations and compressing equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS declines by more than 50K over 3 months and SPX 20-day returns are still positive in >60% of cases, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5%, monetary policy is restrictive, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 3.64% is above 3.5% threshold
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day returns are positive in >50% of trigger instances (FEDFUNDS > 3.5%) over a 5-year rolling window, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rise in retail sales and low unemployment indicates robust consumer demand and tight labor markets, lifting corporate profits and equity prices over short horizons.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 734967 and UNRATE at 4.3% both favorable
failure_condition: If the composite signal (RSAFS rising and UNRATE falling) does not yield mean SPX 5-day return above zero with t-test p<0.05 over a 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: In regimes where consumer price inflation exceeds 2% year-over-year, subsequent federal funds rate increases further tighten financial conditions, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 326.6 implies YoY growth around 2.3%
failure_condition: If in periods with CPIAUCSL YoY > 2%, the correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and SPX 20-day returns is not significantly negative (p>0.05) over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below zero annualized growth signals an impending recession, causing investors to reduce risk exposure and pushing S&P 500 returns negative over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day returns are positive in >60% of instances when INDPRO 3m annualized growth is negative over a 10-year test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with rising retail sales indicate early-cycle demand strength, boosting construction activity and consumer spending, which lifts equity prices over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1246 and RSAFS at 734967 both elevated
failure_condition: If the joint signal of rising HOUST and RSAFS does not produce median SPX 20-day return > 0 across all instances in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4.5% signals a tight labor market, driving wage growth and consumer spending, which supports corporate earnings and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: If SPX 5-day returns are non-positive in >50% of instances when UNRATE < 4.5% over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 3% year-over-year, the economy is in an above-trend expansion, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 level at 24026.8, growth rate to be computed from prior year
failure_condition: If in periods with GDPC1 YoY growth > 3%, SPX 60-day returns are negative more often than positive over a 10-year sample.
